title: Network Layer
description: Covers IP, NAT, DHCP, subnets, and routing algorithms.

questions.prompt: What is the main protocol of the network layer?
Which of the following are reserved IPv4 ranges for private addresses?
Which application-layer protocol dynamically assigns IP addresses to devices on a network (typically run by the gateway router)?
Match each network-layer concept to its description.
right: Translates private addresses to a shared public address using port numbers to distinguish hosts
Constructing routing tables, typically by running a routing algorithm
Moving a packet from a router's input to the appropriate output link
Selecting the routing-table entry whose destination prefix matches the most bits of the target IP

questions.prompt: Which of the following are global (link-state) routing algorithms?
options: Flooding link costs across the network

questions.prompt: Which equation, relating d_x(y) to the d_v(y) of x's neighbours, is the distance vector algorithm based on?
Which of the following are TRUE about NAT?
options: It allows many private IPs to share a single public IP
It uses port numbers at the gateway to distinguish internal hosts
It is considered controversial because port numbers are meant to identify processes, not hosts
It is a permanent fix to IPv4 address exhaustion
It requires every NAT translation to be recorded in a table on the gateway

questions.prompt: In the CIDR notation a.b.c.d/x, what does x represent?
accepted: number of bits in the subnet prefix
number of bits in the subnet mask
